key_insight: HACO used sparse human guidance to transfer a masked generative modeling idea from vision into crystal structure prediction, producing MaskGXT for faster discovery of candidate crystal structures from composition.
engineering_relevance: This suggests AI co-scientists can help engineers design better materials-search algorithms by combining cross-domain model transfer with domain constraints like symmetry and polymorph coverage.
method: Searched generative modeling methods across domains and selected MaskGIT as the CSP framework
Represented crystal structures as discrete tokens and added crystallographic symmetry tokens
Used space-group-stratified sampling and sub-bin coordinate refinement to improve polymorph coverage and geometry precision
evidence_signal: On the MP-20 polymorph split, MaskGXT achieved 79.06% METRe accuracy versus 70.87% for the strongest baseline, and also ranked best on MP-20 and MPTS-52 benchmarks.
watch_item: The approach depends on cheap, fast, and well-aligned validation signals, so performance may be harder to reproduce in settings where evaluation is slower, noisier, or less directly tied to the target property.
paper_title: Discovering Crystal Structure Prediction Algorithms with an AI Co-Scientist
